reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 6** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py (1)
> > `423-455`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ > > **Persist the final trace after review, not before it.** > > In the current `handle()` path, `_generate_plan_traced()` has already written a frozen trace and `all_traces.append(trace)` happens before `_review_plan()` runs. That leaves `accepted`, `review_result`, and `reviewer_model` stuck at their pre-review values, so `_compute_metrics()` underreports review activity and can mark reviewer-rejected attempts as accepted. `payload_data["reviewer_model"]` is also hardcoded instead of using the configured reasoning endpoint or `None`. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In > `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py` > around lines 423 - 455, The frozen trace produced by _generate_plan_traced is > being persisted and appended to all_traces before _review_plan runs, so > accepted/review fields and reviewer_model are stale; change the flow so you run > _review_plan first, update the trace with review_result (accepted flag, issues, > risk_level, reviewer identifier), then persist/append that updated trace to > all_traces (so _compute_metrics sees the final review state). Also replace the > hardcoded payload_data["reviewer_model"] value with the configured reasoning > endpoint (use self._endpoints[EnumModelTier.LOCAL_REASONING].model_id when > present) or None when no reviewer is configured, and ensure payload_data > includes the review_result fields copied from the updated trace. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Inline comments: In `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`: - Around line 394-395: The code currently sets primary_coder_model = "" and stamps metrics with that first-seen value, which misattributes mixed-tier runs; instead compute the summary model from the actual traces (all_traces / traces: list[ModelDispatchTrace])—for example, derive primary_coder_model by aggregating ModelDispatchTrace.endpoint.model_id values (most common or highest-weighted) or, better, emit per-model summaries by grouping traces by endpoint.model_id and recording metrics per group; update any use sites that rely on primary_coder_model (references to primary_coder_model and the metric-emitting code around the dispatch trace aggregation) to use the derived value or per-model groups and avoid leaving it as an empty string for zero-target runs. - Around line 558-564: The token counts are always zero because _call_endpoint() currently returns only message content and _generate_plan_traced() hardcodes prompt_tokens/completion_tokens to 0 while _review_plan() never records review_tokens, so _compute_metrics() only sums zeros; change _call_endpoint() to return the full API response or a usage object (including prompt_tokens and completion_tokens), update _generate_plan_traced() to extract and set prompt_tokens and completion_tokens from that response instead of hardcoding 0, modify _review_plan() to compute and store review_tokens from the review API response, and ensure _compute_metrics() aggregates prompt_tokens, completion_tokens and review_tokens from the stored fields so handle() emits real token totals (refer to functions: handle, _call_endpoint, _generate_plan_traced, _review_plan, _compute_metrics and fields prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, review_tokens). - Around line 543-584: When _call_endpoint() fails you must not return a fabricated plan; instead preserve and emit the trace/gate but propagate the failure to handle() so it can avoid creating a delegation payload and still count the attempt. In the except block that builds ModelQualityGateResult/gate and trace, keep calling _write_trace and _emit_trace_to_bus but then re-raise the original exception (or throw a small ModelDispatchError wrapping exc) rather than continuing to parse raw into plan; update handle() to catch that exception and skip payload creation/total_dispatched increment while still recording the attempt in metrics. - Around line 61-63: The two hardcoded topic constants _DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC and _DELEGATION_METRICS_TOPIC must be removed and loaded from the shared contract the same way _DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC is resolved; update adapter_llm_dispatch.py to read both topic names from contract.yaml (reuse the existing contract loader/utility used for _DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC), replace usages of _DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC and _DELEGATION_METRICS_TOPIC with the values retrieved from the contract, and add the new topic keys to contract.yaml so handlers follow the “no hardcoded topics” guideline. In `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_dod_verify/handlers/handler_dod_verify.py`: - Around line 35-39: The public handler signature was changed and must be restored: reintroduce a RuntimeLocal shim method handle(self, input_data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any] that extracts/pops transport metadata, builds a ModelDodVerifyStartCommand, calls the existing typed implementation (rename current handle(command: ModelDodVerifyStartCommand, ...) -> ModelDodVerifyState to a private helper like _handle_command or keep it and call it from the shim), and finally serializes the returned ModelDodVerifyState to a JSON-serializable dict before returning; ensure you update references to ModelDodVerifyStartCommand, ModelEvidenceCheckResult, and ModelDodVerifyState accordingly so callers using plain dict input (e.g. run_verification tests) continue to work. - Around line 54-59: The current outcome logic sets overall to VERIFIED whenever failed == 0 and checks is non-empty, which incorrectly marks all-skipped checks as VERIFIED; update the conditional in handler_dod_verify.py to set overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.FAILED if failed > 0, overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.SKIPPED if len(checks) == 0 or all(c.status == EnumDodVerifyStatus.SKIPPED for c in checks), and otherwise overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.VERIFIED (or use any(c.status == EnumDodVerifyStatus.VERIFIED for c in checks) to decide VERIFIED); change the branch that computes overall (the block using failed, checks, and EnumDodVerifyStatus) accordingly so completed events reflect SKIPPED when every check was skipped. --- Outside diff comments: In `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`: - Around line 423-455: The frozen trace produced by _generate_plan_traced is being persisted and appended to all_traces before _review_plan runs, so accepted/review fields and reviewer_model are stale; change the flow so you run _review_plan first, update the trace with review_result (accepted flag, issues, risk_level, reviewer identifier), then persist/append that updated trace to all_traces (so _compute_metrics sees the final review state). Also replace the hardcoded payload_data["reviewer_model"] value with the configured reasoning endpoint (use self._endpoints[EnumModelTier.LOCAL_REASONING].model_id when present) or None when no reviewer is configured, and ensure payload_data includes the review_result fields copied from the updated trace. ```

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Move these bus topics into a contract.**

Both event names are hardcoded in handler code, which makes the producer drift-prone against the declared event contract. Resolve them from `contract.yaml` the same way `_DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC` is loaded. As per coding guidelines, `**/nodes/node_*/handlers/*.py`: Never hardcode topics in handler code; declare them in contract.yaml instead.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 61 - 63, The two hardcoded topic constants
_DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC and _DELEGATION_METRICS_TOPIC must be removed and
loaded from the shared contract the same way _DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC is
resolved; update adapter_llm_dispatch.py to read both topic names from
contract.yaml (reuse the existing contract loader/utility used for
_DEFAULT_DELEGATION_TOPIC), replace usages of _DELEGATION_ATTEMPT_TOPIC and
_DELEGATION_METRICS_TOPIC with the values retrieved from the contract, and add
the new topic keys to contract.yaml so handlers follow the “no hardcoded topics”
guideline.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper:4a584f0f-00ed-4204-8d60-9821d9deffba -->

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Don't label the whole run with the first coder model.**

Routing is per ticket, but metrics are stamped with `primary_coder_model`, which is just the first `endpoint.model_id` seen. Mixed-tier runs will therefore misattribute some attempts, and a zero-target run writes `""`. Since each trace already carries its actual coder, derive the summary value from `traces` or emit per-model summaries instead.

Also applies to: 411-412, 488-492

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 394 - 395, The code currently sets primary_coder_model = "" and
stamps metrics with that first-seen value, which misattributes mixed-tier runs;
instead compute the summary model from the actual traces (all_traces / traces:
list[ModelDispatchTrace])—for example, derive primary_coder_model by aggregating
ModelDispatchTrace.endpoint.model_id values (most common or highest-weighted)
or, better, emit per-model summaries by grouping traces by endpoint.model_id and
recording metrics per group; update any use sites that rely on
primary_coder_model (references to primary_coder_model and the metric-emitting
code around the dispatch trace aggregation) to use the derived value or
per-model groups and avoid leaving it as an empty string for zero-target runs.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper:4a584f0f-00ed-4204-8d60-9821d9deffba -->

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Don't turn transport failures into delegation payloads.**

When `_call_endpoint()` throws, this method still builds a fallback plan and returns normally. `handle()` then emits a payload and increments `total_dispatched` for a ticket that never received a model response. Keep the failed trace for observability, but signal failure back to `handle()` so it can skip payload creation while still counting the attempt in metrics.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 543 - 584, When _call_endpoint() fails you must not return a
fabricated plan; instead preserve and emit the trace/gate but propagate the
failure to handle() so it can avoid creating a delegation payload and still
count the attempt. In the except block that builds ModelQualityGateResult/gate
and trace, keep calling _write_trace and _emit_trace_to_bus but then re-raise
the original exception (or throw a small ModelDispatchError wrapping exc) rather
than continuing to parse raw into plan; update handle() to catch that exception
and skip payload creation/total_dispatched increment while still recording the
attempt in metrics.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper:4a584f0f-00ed-4204-8d60-9821d9deffba -->

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**The token totals stay at zero in the current `handle()` path.**

`_call_endpoint()` only returns message content, `_generate_plan_traced()` hardcodes `prompt_tokens=0` / `completion_tokens=0`, and `_review_plan()` never records `review_tokens`. `_compute_metrics()` can only sum those stored fields, so the metrics emitted by `handle()` currently report zero token consumption regardless of the actual API response.

Also applies to: 623-628, 658-666

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`
around lines 558 - 564, The token counts are always zero because
_call_endpoint() currently returns only message content and
_generate_plan_traced() hardcodes prompt_tokens/completion_tokens to 0 while
_review_plan() never records review_tokens, so _compute_metrics() only sums
zeros; change _call_endpoint() to return the full API response or a usage object
(including prompt_tokens and completion_tokens), update _generate_plan_traced()
to extract and set prompt_tokens and completion_tokens from that response
instead of hardcoding 0, modify _review_plan() to compute and store
review_tokens from the review API response, and ensure _compute_metrics()
aggregates prompt_tokens, completion_tokens and review_tokens from the stored
fields so handle() emits real token totals (refer to functions: handle,
_call_endpoint, _generate_plan_traced, _review_plan, _compute_metrics and fields
prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, review_tokens).
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper:4a584f0f-00ed-4204-8d60-9821d9deffba -->

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Restore the RuntimeLocal `handle(dict) -> dict` shim.**

This changes the handler’s public entry point away from the RuntimeLocal contract. A caller that still invokes `handle(input_data)` will now pass a plain dict into `command.correlation_id` / `command.ticket_id`, and this method now returns `ModelDodVerifyState` instead of the JSON-serializable dict the framework expects. `tests/test_golden_chain_dod_verify.py` (Lines 139-182) still uses `run_verification()`, so this regression is easy to miss.

Keep this typed flow in a private helper and reintroduce `handle(self, input_data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]`, matching `src/omnimarket/nodes/node_ticket_pipeline/handlers/handler_ticket_pipeline.py` (Lines 126-145).

Based on learnings, "`handle(self, input_data: dict) -> dict`" is the intentional RuntimeLocal protocol shim, and transport metadata may be popped before building the command model.

Also applies to: 61-74

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_dod_verify/handlers/handler_dod_verify.py` around
lines 35 - 39, The public handler signature was changed and must be restored:
reintroduce a RuntimeLocal shim method handle(self, input_data: dict[str, Any])
-> dict[str, Any] that extracts/pops transport metadata, builds a
ModelDodVerifyStartCommand, calls the existing typed implementation (rename
current handle(command: ModelDodVerifyStartCommand, ...) -> ModelDodVerifyState
to a private helper like _handle_command or keep it and call it from the shim),
and finally serializes the returned ModelDodVerifyState to a JSON-serializable
dict before returning; ensure you update references to
ModelDodVerifyStartCommand, ModelEvidenceCheckResult, and ModelDodVerifyState
accordingly so callers using plain dict input (e.g. run_verification tests)
continue to work.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper:f9ec7f7b-6c61-45b4-bb9c-9de4a7e5eb79 -->

inline_comments.author: coderabbitai[bot]
inline_comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**All-skipped checks currently report `VERIFIED`.**

When every result is `SKIPPED`, `failed == 0` and `len(checks) > 0`, so this falls through to `EnumDodVerifyStatus.VERIFIED`. That marks a ticket as passed even though nothing was actually verified, and the same wrong status is propagated into the completed event.

<details>
<summary>Minimal fix</summary>

```diff
         if failed > 0:
             overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.FAILED
-        elif len(checks) == 0:
-            overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.SKIPPED
-        else:
+        elif verified > 0:
             overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.VERIFIED
+        else:
+            overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.SKIPPED
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/omnimarket/nodes/node_dod_verify/handlers/handler_dod_verify.py` around
lines 54 - 59, The current outcome logic sets overall to VERIFIED whenever
failed == 0 and checks is non-empty, which incorrectly marks all-skipped checks
as VERIFIED; update the conditional in handler_dod_verify.py to set overall =
EnumDodVerifyStatus.FAILED if failed > 0, overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.SKIPPED
if len(checks) == 0 or all(c.status == EnumDodVerifyStatus.SKIPPED for c in
checks), and otherwise overall = EnumDodVerifyStatus.VERIFIED (or use
any(c.status == EnumDodVerifyStatus.VERIFIED for c in checks) to decide
VERIFIED); change the branch that computes overall (the block using failed,
checks, and EnumDodVerifyStatus) accordingly so completed events reflect SKIPPED
when every check was skipped.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper:f9ec7f7b-6c61-45b4-bb9c-9de4a7e5eb79 -->

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough This pull request introduces comprehensive dispatch tracing and metrics collection to the LLM dispatch system. New Pydantic models capture per-attempt trace data and aggregate metrics. The handler refactors a verification workflow to use a single typed entry point, simplifying state construction. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Dispatch Tracing Models**
`src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/models/model_dispatch_trace.py`, `src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/models/model_dispatch_metrics.py`, `src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/models/__init__.py`|New Pydantic models define per-attempt dispatch traces (`ModelDispatchTrace`) with quality-gate and optional review results, plus aggregate metrics (`ModelDispatchMetrics`) including token totals, failure rates, and ticket counts. Both models are immutable and forbid extra fields. Module exports updated to include new public symbols.| |**Dispatch Handler Enhancement**
`src/omnimarket/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/handlers/adapter_llm_dispatch.py`|Added dispatch observability layer with `_generate_plan_traced()` that creates traces for all generation attempts, writes them to `.onex_state/dispatch-traces/`, and conditionally emits Kafka events. New `_compute_metrics()` and `_write_metrics()` aggregate attempt data into dispatch metrics written to `.onex_state/dispatch-metrics/{correlation_id}.json`. Extended `__init__` with optional `state_dir` parameter. Module `__all__` updated to export helper functions.| |**Handler Refactoring**
`src/omnimarket/nodes/node_dod_verify/handlers/handler_dod_verify.py`|Simplified `HandlerDodVerify` by replacing separate `start()` and `run_checks()` methods with a single typed `handle(command, evidence_results=None) -> ModelDodVerifyState` entry point. Removed generic `dict[str, Any]` shim and consolidated state construction logic. `run_verification()` now delegates to the unified `handle()` method.| |**Test Coverage**
`tests/unit/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/test_dispatch_trace.py`, `tests/unit/nodes/node_build_loop_orchestrator/test_dispatch_metrics.py`|Comprehensive unit tests for trace and metrics models, including Pydantic validation, immutability, field constraints, and I/O operations (`_write_trace`, `_write_metrics`). Integration tests verify end-to-end tracing and metrics generation in `AdapterLlmDispatch.handle()`, covering success/failure scenarios and confirming Kafka no-op behavior when disabled.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client as Client participant Dispatch as AdapterLlmDispatch participant LLM as LLM Endpoint participant Trace as Trace Writer participant Metrics as Metrics Computer participant Emit as Event Emitter participant FS as File System Client->>Dispatch: handle() [non-dry run] loop For each ticket Dispatch->>Dispatch: _generate_plan_traced() Dispatch->>LLM: _call_endpoint() LLM-->>Dispatch: response (or error) Dispatch->>Dispatch: parse & quality-gate Dispatch->>Dispatch: create ModelDispatchTrace Dispatch->>Trace: trace data Trace->>FS: write .onex_state/dispatch-traces/{id}-{ticket}-attempt-{n}.json Dispatch->>Dispatch: collect trace for metrics end Dispatch->>Metrics: _compute_metrics(all traces) Metrics->>Dispatch: ModelDispatchMetrics (totals, rates) Dispatch->>FS: _write_metrics() → .onex_state/dispatch-metrics/{correlation_id}.json Dispatch->>Emit: _emit_metrics_to_bus() alt KAFKA_ENABLED Emit->>Emit: emit onex.evt.omnimarket.delegation-metrics.v1 end Dispatch-->>Client: result ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~45 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 *Hops with glee* > Traces now capture each attempt with care, > Metrics aggregate the story in the air— > JSON files bloom in `.onex_state` so fair, > While Kafka hops along, if enabled there! > Quality gates dance, and reviews declare— > A dispatch symphony, lovingly laid bare! 🥕
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 2 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 41.38% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (2 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :---------------: | :------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title clearly and concisely summarizes the main change: adding dispatch metrics summary functionality to the codebase. |
